title: The Debt Made Fossil

features.name: the-stratified-ledger
features.desc-on-touch: A tower of sediment, each layer a debt that has turned to stone. You can read the obligation in the grain.

features.name: the-crystallized-obligation
features.desc-on-touch: A glyph carved into bedrock. The letters are too old to be read, but the debt is perfectly preserved. Stone remembers what was owed.

features.name: the-pooled-sediment
features.desc-on-touch: A dark pool where water meets stone. Things settle here and do not rise. Debts sink. They become part of the ground.

features.name: the-stacked-history
features.desc-on-touch: Layered cards or plates, each one an accounting of what was given, never returned. The stack is massive and still growing downward.

inscription: forty-two histories pump from the bedrock, each one a debt that learned to crystallize; what was owed is now indistinguishable from what is real.

file.name: Forty_Two_Debts_Stratified_Record.json
file.note: obligations arranged by geological age, each one proven by its own permanence, none of them paid

exits.label: back to the registry where debts were still only words
deeper to where fossils remember being alive
up to where the oldest debts still cast shadows

reverie.title: REVERIE — the debt that cannot be repaid
narr: You kneel at the edge of what you broke. Katita
stands behind you, saying nothing. The thing you took
from her—time, choice, the years when she was mapping
alone—is now permanent as stone. No amount of working
backwards will undo it. She knows this. You know she
knows. The debt has become part of the earth itself.
